summary: High-converting landing page for 'Organized Digital Life' brand promoting a 2026 Rainbow Digital Planner for iPad & GoodNotes. Minimal, elegant, Pinterest-friendly design with soft beige background and lavender accents. Target audience: women aged 18–35 interested in aesthetic digital planning. Single conversion goal with mobile-first optimization.
architectural_notes: Single-page landing design with no navigation bar or footer
Mobile-first responsive layout optimized for Pinterest traffic
Soft beige background with lavender accents and clean typography
High-contrast CTA button repeated twice (hero and final sections)
Conversion-focused design with single goal and minimal distractions
No trust lines or subscription messaging allowed under CTA buttons

existing_features.title: Implement hero section with large headline 'Stay Organized in 2026 Without Overcomplicating Your Life', subheadline 'The All-in-One Digital Planner for iPad & GoodNotes', centered iPad mockup showing colorful weekly layout, prominent rounded CTA button labeled 'Get The Planner Now', and trust line 'Instant Download – No Subscription Required' below the button

Create benefits section with title 'Why You'll Love This Planner' and 4 benefit cards with check icons displaying: 'Weekly + Daily Pages', 'Hyperlinked Navigation', 'Habit & Goal Tracking', and 'Clean, Aesthetic Design'
Implement visual preview section showing 3 planner preview images: weekly layout, daily layout, and habit tracker page
Create 'Who It's For' section with bullet list containing: 'Students', 'Busy Professionals', 'Entrepreneurs', and 'Anyone who wants clarity & structure'
Implement final CTA section with headline 'Start Planning Smarter Today' and large centered CTA button
Apply minimal, elegant, Pinterest-friendly design with soft beige background, lavender accents, clean typography, and generous white space throughout the landing page
Implement mobile-first responsive design with no navigation bar, no social media icons, no footer distractions, optimized for minimal scrolling and single conversion goal
Add subtle fade-in animations on scroll for sections to enhance user experience without overwhelming the conversion-focused layout
Ensure CTA buttons use high contrast design to stand out prominently, with repeated CTA placement in hero and final sections for maximum conversion optimization
Render generated planner preview images (iPad mockup, weekly layout, daily layout, habit tracker page) as static assets in frontend/public/assets/generated
Remove the trust line 'Instant Download – No Subscription Required' from below the CTA button in the hero section
Remove all text content and references to 'Instant Download' from the entire landing page
Remove all text content and references to 'No Subscription Required' from the entire landing page

Refine the Weekly Layout preview image to display a clean aesthetic mockup with a visible iPad frame, soft pastel background, clearly highlighted weekly structure, and removal of any cluttered elements
Render the refined Weekly Layout image as a static asset in frontend/public/assets/generated, replacing the existing weekly layout preview

new_feature_requests.summary: Hero section with headline, subheadline, iPad mockup, and CTA button
Benefits section with 4 benefit cards and check icons
Visual preview section showing 3 planner preview images
Who It's For section with bullet list
Final CTA section with headline and button
Refine Weekly Layout image with clean aesthetic mockup, visible iPad frame, soft pastel background, clear weekly structure, no clutter
Diagnose and resolve the deployment error that occurred during the previous build attempt
Verify that all static assets in frontend/public/assets/generated are properly configured and accessible
Ensure the backend main.mo actor compiles without errors and contains valid Motoko syntax
